---
title: "Financial Health"
subtitle: "Margins, reserves, and revenue mix among the region's larger nonprofits"
---

::: {.key-finding}
<span class="kf-label">Key finding</span>
The region's larger nonprofits are, in aggregate, modestly healthy but
thinly buffered: among **507 organizations with current Form 990 filings**,
the median operating margin is **+5.1%** and median reserves equal **10.7
months of expenses**. Underneath those medians, **one in three ran a deficit**
in its latest fiscal year and **one in five holds less than three months of
operating reserves** — the level commonly considered the minimum safe cushion.
Human services organizations are more exposed: 26% are below the three-month
line.
:::

{{< include _shared.qmd >}}

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| output: false
fin = FileAttachment("data/fin_metrics.csv").csv({typed: true})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| output: false
finByYear = FileAttachment("data/fin_by_year.csv").csv({typed: true})
```

## Who this page covers

Financial-health analysis requires full Form 990 data, so this page covers
active regional organizations with **at least $500,000 in revenue** (plus the
25 largest by assets) that have a 990 or 990-EZ filing from 2021 or later —
507 organizations. Metrics come from each organization's most recent filing.
Definitions and caveats (including why a true "overhead ratio" cannot be
computed from extract data) are on the [Methodology](methodology.qmd) page.

## Revenue distribution

Nonprofit revenue spans five orders of magnitude, so the axis is
logarithmic. Each dot is one organization.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Plot.plot({
  height: 340,
  width: 900,
  marginLeft: 200,
  marginBottom: 45,
  style: {fontSize: "13px"},
  x: {type: "log", label: "Total revenue, most recent filing ($, log scale)", labelAnchor: "center", grid: true, tickFormat: d => d3.format("~s")(d).replace("G", "B")},
  y: {label: null},
  marks: [
    Plot.dot(fin.filter(d => d.totrevenue > 1000), {
      x: "totrevenue",
      y: "subsector_name",
      fill: GU.s1,
      fillOpacity: 0.45,
      r: 3.5,
      tip: true,
      title: d => `${d.name}\n${d.city} · FY${d.tax_prd_yr}\nRevenue: ${fmoney(d.totrevenue)}`
    }),
    Plot.tickX(
      d3.rollups(fin.filter(d => d.totrevenue > 1000), v => d3.median(v, d => d.totrevenue), d => d.subsector_name)
        .map(([subsector_name, med]) => ({subsector_name, med})),
      {x: "med", y: "subsector_name", stroke: GU.red, strokeWidth: 3}
    )
  ]
})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
md`<span style="color:${GU.muted}; font-size:0.85rem;"><span style="color:${GU.s1}">●</span> organization &nbsp;&nbsp;<span style="color:${GU.red}">|</span> category median. Hover any dot for the organization.</span>`
```

## Operating margins

Share of latest-year revenue left after expenses. Margins cluster near
break-even by design — nonprofits price to mission, not profit — but
persistent deficits erode reserves.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| output: false
marginData = fin.filter(d => d.margin != null && isFinite(d.margin) && d.margin > -1 && d.margin < 1)
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Plot.plot({
  height: 320,
  width: 900,
  marginLeft: 55,
  marginBottom: 45,
  style: {fontSize: "13px"},
  x: {label: "Operating margin (latest fiscal year)", labelAnchor: "center", tickFormat: ".0%", domain: [-0.6, 0.6]},
  y: {label: "Organizations", grid: true},
  marks: [
    Plot.rectY(marginData, Plot.binX(
      {y: "count"},
      {
        x: "margin",
        interval: 0.05,
        fill: d => d.margin < 0 ? GU.red : GU.s1,
        insetLeft: 1,
        insetRight: 1,
        rx: 2,
        tip: true
      }
    )),
    Plot.ruleX([0], {stroke: GU.ink, strokeWidth: 1.5}),
    Plot.ruleY([0], {stroke: "#c3c2b7"})
  ]
})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
md`<span style="color:${GU.muted}; font-size:0.85rem;"><span style="color:${GU.red}">&#9632;</span> deficit (${fpct(d3.mean(marginData, d => d.margin < 0) * 100)} of organizations) &nbsp;&nbsp;<span style="color:${GU.s1}">&#9632;</span> surplus. Margins beyond ±60% (a handful of foundations with lumpy grant income) are excluded from the chart.</span>`
```

## Operating reserves

Months of expenses covered by net assets — the sector's standard solvency
gauge. Three months is the commonly cited minimum; the red band marks
organizations below it.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| output: false
reserveData = fin.filter(d => d.months_reserves != null && isFinite(d.months_reserves))
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| output: false
reserveBuckets = {
  const buckets = [
    {label: "Negative", test: d => d.months_reserves < 0, atRisk: true},
    {label: "0–3 months", test: d => d.months_reserves >= 0 && d.months_reserves < 3, atRisk: true},
    {label: "3–6 months", test: d => d.months_reserves >= 3 && d.months_reserves < 6, atRisk: false},
    {label: "6–12 months", test: d => d.months_reserves >= 6 && d.months_reserves < 12, atRisk: false},
    {label: "1–3 years", test: d => d.months_reserves >= 12 && d.months_reserves < 36, atRisk: false},
    {label: "3+ years", test: d => d.months_reserves >= 36, atRisk: false}
  ];
  return buckets.map(b => ({bucket: b.label, atRisk: b.atRisk, n: reserveData.filter(b.test).length}));
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Plot.plot({
  height: 300,
  width: 900,
  marginLeft: 55,
  marginBottom: 45,
  style: {fontSize: "13px"},
  x: {label: "Net assets as months of expenses", labelAnchor: "center", domain: reserveBuckets.map(d => d.bucket)},
  y: {label: "Organizations", grid: true},
  marks: [
    Plot.barY(reserveBuckets, {
      x: "bucket",
      y: "n",
      fill: d => d.atRisk ? GU.red : GU.s1,
      rx: 3,
      insetLeft: 5,
      insetRight: 5,
      tip: true,
      title: d => `${d.bucket}: ${fnum(d.n)} organizations (${fpct(d.n / reserveData.length * 100)})`
    }),
    Plot.textY(reserveBuckets, {
      x: "bucket", y: "n", text: d => fnum(d.n), dy: -8, fill: GU.ink, fontSize: 12
    }),
    Plot.ruleY([0], {stroke: "#c3c2b7"})
  ]
})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
md`<span style="color:${GU.muted}; font-size:0.85rem;"><span style="color:${GU.red}">&#9632;</span> below the three-month minimum cushion (${fpct(d3.mean(reserveData, d => d.months_reserves < 3) * 100)} of organizations).</span>`
```

## Size vs. cushion

Do bigger organizations hold bigger buffers? Only weakly — thin reserves
appear at every size. Deficit-running organizations (red) with low reserves
(lower half of the chart) are the region's financially fragile institutions.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| output: false
scatterData = fin.filter(d =>
  d.totrevenue > 10000 && d.months_reserves != null && isFinite(d.months_reserves) &&
  d.months_reserves > -12 && d.months_reserves < 120 && d.margin != null
)
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Plot.plot({
  height: 420,
  width: 900,
  marginLeft: 55,
  marginBottom: 45,
  style: {fontSize: "13px"},
  x: {type: "log", label: "Total revenue ($, log scale)", labelAnchor: "center", tickFormat: d => d3.format("~s")(d).replace("G", "B"), grid: true},
  y: {label: "Months of reserves", grid: true},
  color: {
    legend: true,
    domain: ["Surplus", "Deficit"],
    range: [GU.s1, GU.red]
  },
  marks: [
    Plot.ruleY([3], {stroke: GU.muted, strokeDasharray: "4,4"}),
    Plot.text([{x: 15000, y: 3}], {x: "x", y: "y", text: ["3-month minimum"], dy: -8, textAnchor: "start", fill: GU.muted, fontSize: 11}),
    Plot.dot(scatterData, {
      x: "totrevenue",
      y: "months_reserves",
      fill: d => d.margin < 0 ? "Deficit" : "Surplus",
      fillOpacity: 0.55,
      r: 4,
      tip: true,
      title: d => `${d.name}\n${d.city} · ${d.subsector_name} · FY${d.tax_prd_yr}\nRevenue: ${fmoney(d.totrevenue)}\nMargin: ${fpct(d.margin * 100)}\nReserves: ${d3.format(".1f")(d.months_reserves)} months`
    }),
    Plot.ruleY([0], {stroke: "#c3c2b7"})
  ]
})
```

::: {.chart-note}
Reserves above 10 years and below −1 year are clipped from the chart (mostly
endowed foundations and a few organizations in wind-down). Hover any dot for
the organization.
:::

## Revenue mix: earned vs. contributed

Contribution reliance — the share of revenue from donations and grants —
shapes risk. Organizations funded mostly by contributions ride donor and
grant cycles; fee-funded organizations ride demand.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| output: false
mixData = fin.filter(d => d.contrib_share != null && d.contrib_share >= 0 && d.contrib_share <= 1)
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Plot.plot({
  height: 300,
  width: 900,
  marginLeft: 55,
  marginBottom: 45,
  style: {fontSize: "13px"},
  x: {label: "Contributions & grants as share of revenue", labelAnchor: "center", tickFormat: ".0%"},
  y: {label: "Organizations", grid: true},
  marks: [
    Plot.rectY(mixData, Plot.binX(
      {y: "count"},
      {x: "contrib_share", interval: 0.1, fill: GU.s1, insetLeft: 1, insetRight: 1, rx: 2, tip: true}
    )),
    Plot.ruleY([0], {stroke: "#c3c2b7"})
  ]
})
```

::: {.chart-note}
The distribution is U-shaped: the region's nonprofits are mostly either
donation-funded charities (right) or fee/program-funded operations such as
health systems, housing, and credit-counseling services (left), with fewer
blended models between.
:::

## Financial detail: every covered organization

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof finCounty = Inputs.select(
  ["All counties"].concat(Array.from(new Set(fin.map(d => d.county))).sort()),
  {label: "County"}
)
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof finSearch = Inputs.search(
  fin.filter(d => finCounty === "All counties" || d.county === finCounty),
  {placeholder: "Search names…", columns: ["name", "city"]}
)
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Inputs.table(
  finSearch
    .sort((a, b) => d3.descending(a.totrevenue, b.totrevenue))
    .map(d => ({
      Name: d.name,
      County: d.county,
      FY: String(d.tax_prd_yr),
      Revenue: fmoney(d.totrevenue),
      Expenses: fmoney(d.totfuncexpns),
      "Margin": d.margin != null ? fpct(d.margin * 100) : "—",
      "Months reserves": d.months_reserves != null && isFinite(d.months_reserves) ? d3.format(".1f")(d.months_reserves) : "—",
      "Contrib. share": d.contrib_share != null ? fpct(Math.min(Math.max(d.contrib_share, 0), 1) * 100) : "—"
    })),
  {rows: 15, layout: "auto"}
)
```

::: {.chart-note}
Source: IRS Form 990/990-EZ extracts via ProPublica Nonprofit Explorer API,
latest filing per organization (2021 or later).
:::
